concerns.text: T009 (Phase 2) and T043 (Phase 4) both implement missing_invariant_flags in code/data/validator.py. This creates duplicate work across phases. T043 should be removed or merged with T009. Same file, same functionality, different phases violates producer-before-consumer ordering.

T043a implements ambiguous classification flagging in code/data/validator.py but is in Phase 4 (US4). T009-T010 implement validator flagging in Phase 2 (Foundational). T043a should be moved to Phase 2 as part of the foundational validator infrastructure, not split across phases.

T029 says 'Implement flagging system' but T009-T010 already implement this in Phase 2. T029 should be 'Apply/Use flags' not 'Implement flagging system'. Producer (T009-T010) must precede consumer (T029), but T029's description incorrectly claims implementation.

T040-T041 validate hyperbolic volume against KnotInfo but are in Phase 5 (US3 Regression). Volume validation is a data quality task that should occur in Phase 3 (US1 Download/Parse) with T019-T020, not after regression models are fitted. Consumer (validation) should be with producer (data download).

T017 generates dataset size report but T018 saves the data files. If T017 needs to count records from saved files, T017 should come after T018. Currently ambiguous whether counts come from Knot Atlas metadata or saved files.
T057 (unit tests) is in Phase N (Polish) but unit tests should be written alongside implementation per TDD. Consider distributing unit tests across US1-US4 phases rather than lumping at the end.

Reviewer-driven tasks (T067-T072) are distributed across phases but should be explicitly linked to their source (simulated reviewer feedback) in task descriptions for audit traceability. Currently appears as scope creep without provenance.
